skill.name: java-maven-helper
skill.description: Maven build system, dependency management, and Java project configuration assistance for Java projects.
skill.summary: Configure, troubleshoot, and optimize Maven builds for Java applications.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many shell command, network URL, filesystem, and weak-cryptography patterns in SKILL.md. AI review found most command and URL alerts are documentation examples, and the weak-cryptography alerts are false positives from ordinary words and XML tokens. The remaining risk is medium because the skill recommends destructive local Maven cache deletion, includes executable Maven commands, and shows a plaintext repository password example.

medium_findings.title: Destructive Maven Cache Cleanup Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill includes rm -rf commands for paths under ~/.m2. This is a legitimate Maven troubleshooting pattern, but it can delete the entire local Maven repository and cause data loss or expensive rebuilds if copied without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact destructive commands are present in troubleshooting sections. Context shows repair guidance rather than malware, so the risk is real but not blocking.

medium_findings.title: Plaintext Repository Password Example
medium_findings.description: The settings.xml sample includes a plaintext password value. Users may copy this pattern into real Maven settings and store credentials insecurely.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The password is clearly an example, but it appears in a Maven server authentication block. This can encourage unsafe credential handling if reused.

medium_findings.title: Executable Maven Commands With Build and Deployment Side Effects
medium_findings.description: The skill provides many mvn commands, including deploy, package, purge-local-repository, and CI execution examples. These are expected for a Maven helper, but they can download dependencies, run plugins, publish artifacts, or modify local project state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are standard Maven operations and align with the skill purpose. The risk is operational because Maven builds can execute plugins and affect local or remote repositories.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs Are Benign
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alerts point to XML namespaces, Maven Central, Apache license documentation, and example project URLs. No evidence found of data exfiltration or suspicious callback endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are embedded in XML examples or public documentation references. They do not collect data or instruct unauthorized network calls.

low_findings.title: SSH SCM URL Is Example Configuration
low_findings.description: The SSH protocol URL appears in a sample Maven SCM block for a placeholder GitHub repository. No evidence found that the skill initiates SSH connections.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The SSH URL is inside XML configuration documentation. It is not executable code and uses placeholder repository coordinates.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts match ordinary text and XML tokens such as description, includes, and descriptorRefs. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar cryptographic implementation guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows non-cryptographic Maven documentation content. The static detector appears to have matched substrings rather than real algorithms.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Deletion Under Home Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: The troubleshooting guide recommends rm -rf against ~/.m2 paths. This should be presented with caution because it deletes local cache content outside the project workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly present and target hidden directories in the user home. The Maven context is legitimate but still destructive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Plaintext Credential Placeholder
dangerous_patterns.description: The Maven settings example includes a password field with a literal placeholder value. This can lead users to place secrets directly in configuration files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The value is an example credential in a real Maven configuration structure. It is not a leaked secret, but it is unsafe guidance if copied.

content.user_title: Configure Maven Builds for Java Projects
content.value_statement: Maven projects often fail because dependency versions, plugins, profiles, and repository settings are hard to inspect. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical Maven guidance for project setup, dependency management, and build troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Explains standard Maven project structure and common lifecycle commands.
Provides pom.xml examples for dependencies, plugins, profiles, repositories, and multi-module builds.
Guides dependency tree analysis, version management, exclusions, and BOM usage.
Helps troubleshoot missing dependencies, version conflicts, slow builds, and corrupted local repositories.
Shows CI examples for Maven builds in GitHub Actions and GitLab CI.
Summarizes Maven best practices for build configuration, testing, and dependency hygiene.
limitations: Does not verify dependency versions against live Maven Central metadata by itself.
Includes shell commands that require user review before execution.
Cannot guarantee that generated pom.xml snippets fit every framework or corporate repository policy.
Some sample versions may become outdated as the Java ecosystem changes.

use_cases.title: Start a New Maven Service
use_cases.description: Create a clean Maven project layout with a working pom.xml, Java version settings, tests, and common plugins.

use_cases.title: Resolve Dependency Problems
use_cases.description: Use dependency tree analysis, exclusions, scopes, and dependencyManagement to fix conflicts or missing artifacts.

use_cases.title: Standardize Build Pipelines
use_cases.description: Add Maven build, test, coverage, caching, and artifact steps to GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Maven Project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use java-maven-helper to outline a simple Maven project for a Java 17 command-line app. Include project structure, required pom.xml sections, and test setup.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Dependency Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use java-maven-helper to add a new library to my Maven project. Explain the right scope, where to place the dependency, and how to verify the dependency tree.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Build Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: Use java-maven-helper to diagnose this Maven error. Explain likely causes, safe commands to run first, and any risky cleanup steps that need confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multi-Module Build
prompt_templates.prompt: Use java-maven-helper to design a multi-module Maven build for core, API, web, and CLI modules. Include parent dependency management, plugin management, and CI guidance.

output_examples.input: I need a Maven setup for a Java 17 web service with unit tests.
output_examples.output: A structured recommendation covering project layout, Java compiler settings, Spring Boot dependency management, unit test dependencies, and build plugin choices.

output_examples.input: My build fails because two libraries bring different Guava versions.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting plan that uses dependency tree analysis, identifies the conflicting path, and recommends dependencyManagement or exclusions.

output_examples.input: Create a CI plan for Maven tests and coverage.
output_examples.output: A build pipeline outline with checkout, JDK setup, Maven cache, compile, test, coverage report, and artifact publishing steps.

best_practices: Review every shell command before running it, especially commands that delete files or deploy artifacts.
Use dependencyManagement or BOMs to control versions across modules.
Keep credentials out of plain settings files when a secure secret store is available.
anti_patterns: Do not copy sample passwords or repository URLs into production settings without replacing them securely.
Do not delete the entire local Maven repository before trying narrower dependency repair steps.
Do not add dependencies without checking scope, transitive conflicts, and version ownership.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps configure Maven projects, manage dependencies, troubleshoot builds, and document common Maven commands.

faq.question: Can it create a complete pom.xml?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides complete and partial pom.xml examples for common Java project patterns.

faq.question: Does it run Maven commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides command guidance. Users should review commands before execution.

faq.question: Is it useful for multi-module projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes parent and child module examples with shared dependency management.

faq.question: Can it fix corrupted local Maven caches?
faq.answer: It suggests Maven cleanup approaches, but destructive deletion commands should be used only after review.

faq.question: Does it check the latest dependency versions?
faq.answer: Not by itself. Use Maven version plugins or live repository lookup when current version accuracy matters.
